title: Slow Roasted Ethiopian Turkey Mince Tibs
description: A roasted, velvety, and nostalgic Ethiopian dish featuring turkey mince in a lacquer glaze, perfect for a vegetarian friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the turkey mince, onion, garlic, ginger, and berbere spice, mixing well.
Transfer the mixture to a roasting pan and brush with lacquer glaze.
Drizzle the olive oil over the mixture and cover with aluminum foil.
Roast the mixture in the preheated oven for 2 hours.
Remove the foil and continue roasting for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the pan from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Serve the Slow Roasted Ethiopian Turkey Mince Tibs hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs.
Traditionally, tibs is served with injera bread, but it can also be served with rice or roasted vegetables.
